details: Compound 5 mp 63–64 °C confirmed verbatim in experimental section; DOI 10.3390/71000767 present.
Compound 4b mp 156–157 °C confirmed; paper uses 'dimetyl' (typo) but IUPAC dimethyl is the standardized correct name.
Hesperidin Table 1 Tm Exp. = 532.77 K, conversion to 259.62 °C arithmetically correct.
Compound 6 (Diethyl ((E)-4-((Adamantan-1-yl)amino)-4-oxobut-2-en-1-yl)phosphonate) Mp 115–118 °C confirmed in Section 3.1.4.

Table II row 25 shows '300°C EtOH 73' with NO decomp. annotation; compounds 9 and 10 in same table carry explicit 'decomp.' tags but row 25 does not, so property should be melting_point per OVERLAY default, not decomposition.
Compound 7b M.p. 173-175 °C confirmed verbatim in experimental section.
Compound 5a Mp 280–281 °C confirmed in Section 3.4.
Compound 5a mp 179 °C (dec) confirmed; decomposition property correctly tagged due to (dec) annotation.

Compound 3c Mp: 214–216 °C confirmed in experimental section.
Compound 16b m.p. 289-290 °C confirmed verbatim.
Compound 4e m.p. 135-139 °C confirmed verbatim in experimental section.
Compound 4e mp 128.9°C confirmed in experimental section (also matches Table 1).
Compound 1j Table 1 M.p. column shows 205-207; X=Cl R=4-isoC3H7 substituents match the IUPAC name.
Compound 9h M.p. = 207-208 °C confirmed in SI; PDF wrap split the M.p. line but value is correct.
Vincadifformine-Nb-oxide m.p. 160°C (dec.) confirmed in experimental section; decomposition property correct.
Compound 17 (N-(2-Methylbenzyl)-...-quinolone-3-carboxamide) m.p: 240–242 °C confirmed in Section 4.2.12.

Paper labels compound 5b as '2(N)-(2,2'-diamino-4,4'-dimethoxybenzophenone)-1,4-naphthoquinone'; CSV name is '3-(2-amino-4,4'-dimethoxybenzophenone)-...' with different attachment position (3- vs 2(N)-) and only one amino instead of two. Value 214 °C is correct for compound 5b.
Compound 6a mp 103–105 °C confirmed in experimental section.
Table 2 T_onset for [Glu(Osec-Bu)2][HCl] = 181.5 °C; OVERLAY classifies T_onset (onset of thermal degradation, TGA) as decomposition.

Compound 4f mp 222–225 °C confirmed in experimental section.
Table 5 'BP (Exp.)' column for 3-me-3-hexanol = 142.4; experimental literature compilation correctly tagged measured.
